mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate above 3.7% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals a weakening supply cycle that boosts housing prices and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP above 2.1% signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in initial jobless claims above 250k signals a weakening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index below 2.8% signals a weakening monetary cycle that boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening monetary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4.5% signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
